headline: Apple Inc. Emphasizes Design Commitment Amidst High-Profile Exits
summary: In the wake of several significant departures from its design team, Apple underscored its dedication to design during its recent iPhone 17 keynote presentation.

headline: Synopsys, Chewy, And The Trade Desk Are Among Top 10 Large Cap Losers Last Week (Sep. 8- Sep. 12): Are The Others In Your Portfolio?
summary: Earnings misses, weak guidance, and downgrades drove large-cap decliners—led by Synopsys, Chewy, and The Trade Desk—while biotech trial updates and mixed analyst calls pressured others.

headline: China Considers Ban On Flush Door Handles In Cars: Here's Why
summary: China considers ban on flush door handles due to safety risks, like trapping passengers during power loss. Tesla has faced issues too.

headline: Michael Saylor's Bitcoin-Only Prophecy Not Aging Well As Wall Street Embraces $27 Billion In Ethereum ETFs, Buzz Builds Around XRP And Even Dogecoin
summary: Michael Saylor may now seem comfortable with the rise and adoption of the broader crypto ecosystem, but just over a year ago, he was every bit the staunch Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) maximalist.

headline: Warner Bros, Oracle, And Micron Technology Are Among The Top 10 Large-Cap Gainers Last Week (Sep. 8 - Sep. 12): Are The Others In Your Portfolio?
summary: Deal buzz, analyst upgrades, and AI momentum drove top weekly gainers—led by Warner Bros., Oracle, and Micron—across large-cap tech and media.

headline: Musk Confronts Microsoft CEO Over Alleged Employee Remarks On Charlie Kirk's Assassination
Financial Times Reported Earlier, Tesla Chair Robyn Denholm Said $1T Stock Offer To Elon Musk Justified, Due To His Superhuman Effort And Ability To Achieve Impossible Things
Consumer Tech News (September 8 – September 12): Rally Rolls On As iPhone 17 Underwhelms, Microsoft Bets On AI Chips, Oracle Reports Q1
summary: Fed-cut optimism kept markets rallying as iPhone 17 underwhelmed, Oracle posted mixed Q1, Microsoft doubled down on AI chips, and macro signals stayed muddled.

headline: FDA Approves Hypertension Alerts On Apple Watch, iPhone Air's Chinese Launch Delayed And More: This Week In Appleverse
summary: Apple received FDA approval for hypertension alerts on the Apple Watch Ultra 3, delayed the iPhone Air's Chinese launch, and faced a dip in App Store revenue growth.

headline: Elon Musk's Two-Word Response, Trump Administration's Patent Share, Palantir CEO's China Warning And More: This Week In Politics
summary: Elon Musk clashed with the U.S. Department of Energy over its remarks on renewable energy, emphasizing the need for large-scale batteries.

headline: Google Sued By Rolling Stone, Billboard Owner Penske Media Over AI Overviews Feature
summary: Penske Media sues Alphabet's Google over AI summaries, claiming reduced website traffic and revenue.

headline: Tesla's Stock Surge Driven By Anticipated Q3 Delivery Outperformance, Says Gary Black: 'Let's Not Kid Ourselves'
summary: Tesla stock surges due to anticipation of exceeding 3Q delivery targets despite challenges.

headline: Apple Dominates With iPhone 17 Pre-Orders In China, Delays iPhone Air Launch Over eSIM Approval
summary: Apple's iPhone 17 dominated pre-orders on JD.com, with strong Wall Street price target upgrades following its launch.

headline: Bill Gates Backs Utah Geothermal Plant As Fervo Energy Targets 500 MW Capacity At Cape Station
summary: Bill Gates and Breakthrough Energy support Fervo Energy's Cape Station project, aiming to generate 500 MW of geothermal power.

headline: Eric Jackson Taps Drake For Celebrity Marketing To Fuel Opendoor's Growth Following Leadership Overhaul
summary: Eric Jackson's strategy for Opendoor includes leveraging celebrity marketing, with Canadian rapper Drake promoting the company to his followers.

headline: Opendoor Chairman Keith Rabois Calls Company 'Bloated,' Says Only 200 Of 1,400 Employees Needed: Promises Merit-Driven Overhaul
summary: Returning to OPEN board as chairman, Keith Rabois plans to cut 85% of the workforce and revamp culture. Tech layoffs surge amid AI push.

headline: Trump To Seal US-UK Tech Pact On AI, Chips And Quantum Computing During London Visit: Report
summary: The U.S. and UK are set to sign a major tech agreement focusing on AI, semiconductors, and quantum computing.
